context.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 17?
context.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 17?

decision.primary_crux: The move from a prior fair-value center near 0.79 to a market price of 0.84 is best treated as repricing rather than a broken thesis: BTC is still more likely than not to close above $72,000 on the April 17 noon ET Binance minute, but the market is now above bounded fair value for a single-minute settlement with still-meaningful 2-3% downside-path risk.
secondary_cruxes: There is no contract ambiguity carrying the decision; the disagreement is almost entirely about timing and exact-minute fragility.
Current spot above 74k preserves the directional Yes case, but does not eliminate the possibility of a normal drawdown landing on the settlement minute.
No new bounded evidence materially improves confidence in near-term catalyst or volatility risk, so the market move mostly weakens prior value.

valuation.compression_reason: Fresh same-venue spot verification preserved the Yes case and reduced room for a large bearish fade, but did not justify lifting fair value enough to match the repriced market.

portfolio_constraints: Do not add fresh exposure at the updated market price.
Treat exact-minute Binance settlement risk as the dominant execution constraint.
Reopen only if price cheapens materially or a fresher near-settlement Binance check materially improves confidence in the cushion.

bands.name: max_enter
bands.notes: Would require a much cheaper price and still a fresh Binance check near settlement.

bands.name: scaled_enter
bands.notes: Potential value zone only if same-venue state remains strong and exact-minute risk is not worsening.

bands.name: hold
bands.notes: Bounded fair-value zone; maintain watch-only hold posture.

bands.name: trim
bands.notes: Above fair value for a one-minute settlement contract with meaningful path dependence.

bands.name: exit
bands.notes: Avoid or exit at rich prices because exact-minute downside tail dominates residual uncertainty.

thesis_breakers: BTC falls materially back toward 72k-73k on Binance before the Apr 17 fixing window.
A fresh downside catalyst or volatility shock materially raises the odds of a sub-72k settlement minute.
Binance begins underperforming broader spot materially ahead of settlement.
time_breakers: A fresh direct Binance observation closer to Apr 17 12:00 ET should supersede this packet before any action.
The actual Apr 17 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute close fully resolves and obsoletes this judgment.
market_structure_breakers: Market price moves materially again without corresponding same-venue spot support, changing the value comparison.
Binance operational or microstructure issues emerge near settlement.
reversal_conditions: Upgrade only if price becomes materially cheaper relative to fair value or if a fresh pre-fix Binance check shows a meaningfully larger cushion.
Downgrade if BTC loses cushion or if downside volatility or catalyst risk increases.
Do not auto-reverse from this packet.

key_uncertainties: The true probability of a 2-3% downside move landing exactly at the settlement minute.
Whether the market already embeds fresher venue-specific information than the bounded package contains.
How realized volatility will evolve in the final pre-settlement window.
what_would_change_my_mind: A fresh Binance check near settlement showing sustained trade well above 74k would move me somewhat closer to market.
A move toward 72k-73k or evidence of elevated downside catalyst risk would lower fair value materially.
A materially cheaper market price with unchanged Binance cushion would create a cleaner edge.
reasons_to_pass: The current market price is above bounded fair value.
This is a one-minute single-venue settlement contract where path risk matters more than broad BTC direction.
Any edge is too small and fragile to justify authorization.

audit.one_sentence_rationale: BTC is still more likely than not to finish above $72,000 on the April 17 Binance noon minute, but the market's move to 0.84 pushes price above bounded fair value centered near 0.79, so the disciplined output remains watch-only because exact-minute downside-path risk still consumes the edge.
audit.notes: This refresh is mostly a valuation update: same directional Yes, weaker execution case after repricing.
